description: Approximate analysis regions tied to publicly documented travel corridors or municipal trail networks. These are bounding areas for remote-sensing workflows, not surveyed trail centrelines.
disclaimer: Exact subsistence routes are often sensitive or unmapped. Do not treat these polygons as authoritative trail geometry. Prefer official community or government layers when available and permitted.

corridors.summary: All-season public highway (Inuvik–Tuktoyaktuk). AOI is a ~tens-of-km bounding patch along the documented corridor midpoint—not the official road centreline.
Publicly described mountaineering / park travel corridor (Akshayuk Pass, Auyuittuq National Park). AOI is a coarse box around published pass coordinates.
Hamlet-scale box covering terrain where the City of Iqaluit publishes a snowmobile trail map. This polygon is NOT traced from that map—download the municipal map for official routes.
Coastal travel / hunting access region near Kugluktuk (see feature properties in GeoJSON).
Kitikmeot hub on Victoria Island south coast; regional snow/ice/travel context (public location sources).
Kivalliq Hudson Bay coast near Rankin Inlet; pair with Nov 2023 storm reporting (optical RS limited that season).
West Hudson Bay coast near Arviat; same storm period as Rankin in public reports.
Pangnirtung fjord / Auyuittuq access context; summer optical contrasts.
Belcher Islands / Sanikiluaq sea-ice travel context (public ELOKA sea-ice project references).

default_s2_windows.note: Summer 2023 windows with daylight; adjust per research question and cloud cover.
